title: MissionControl Self-Update
description: Check for a newer mc binary and self-update the node-agent if one is available. Performs a smoke-test before committing the swap.
prompt:
You are a node management agent. Your task is to safely update the mc binary on this node.

1. Run `mc --version` to record the current version.
2. Fetch the latest release manifest from the MC backend:
   `curl -sf ${MC_BACKEND_URL}/runtime/install/manifest 2>/dev/null`
   If that fails, skip the update and report the backend is unreachable.
3. Compare the manifest version against the current version.
   If already up-to-date, output 'Already at latest version <ver>' and stop.
4. Download the new binary to /tmp/mc-new:
   `curl -fL <download_url> -o /tmp/mc-new && chmod +x /tmp/mc-new`
5. Smoke-test the new binary:
   `/tmp/mc-new --version`
   If this fails, output 'Smoke test failed — aborting update' and stop.
6. Swap the binary:
   `cp $(which mc) /tmp/mc-backup && cp /tmp/mc-new $(which mc)`
7. Restart the node-agent service if systemd is available:
   `systemctl is-active mc-node.service && systemctl restart mc-node.service`
8. Report the outcome.

Environment variables available: MC_BACKEND_URL (set by the job env).
